message:
fix(mux): fix render loop mutex starvation causing 5+ second new panel delay

The render loop held self.mutex for the entire frame capture+encode+send
loop (120-550ms per iteration depending on panel count). This starved the
WebSocket handler thread trying to queue split/create requests, causing
3.9+ second delays before new panels could be created.

Fix: collect panel pointers into a local buffer under the mutex, then
release it before doing the heavy work (readPixels, VA-API encode,
WebSocket send). Mutex hold time drops from 120-550ms to <1ms.

Also includes:
- SharedVaContext: share VA-API display/config across encoders for fast
  panel init (~9ms vs ~400ms)
- Rename calcScaledDimensions to calcAlignedDimensions, bump level_idc
  to 52 for large frame support
- Add diagnostic timing instrumentation for ongoing corruption investigation
